claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Strong new emphasis on privacy / encrypted-state blockchains (Seismic, Aztec, Aleo) as a major theme — not anticipated in the prediction
Specific large token investments newly disclosed: $55M in LayerZero (ZRO), $70M in EIGEN, plus EigenLayer/restaking enthusiasm
Cross-chain interoperability / asset-wrapping (Universal, 'Circle for cryptoassets') as a focus area
Enthusiasm for the new US regulatory regime (celebrating CFTC chair nominations — Brian Quintenz, Patrick McHenry) — concrete pro-regulatory-tailwind stances
Developer-experience / smart-contract abstraction tooling (Halliday Workflow Protocol) as a backed thesis
notes: The prediction's excited sectors (AI×crypto, DePIN, payments) and risk-on builder stance map cleanly onto the window: heavy decentralized-AI/verification content (Gensyn RL swarm, EigenCloud, Seismic, 'decentralize AI → need verification'), Helium DePIN milestones, and Halliday Payments rails, plus multiple large infrastructure token investments confirming risk-on. Solana confirmed via hosting Toly, the Solana podcast, and SolanaConf. Both 'concerned' claims are absent — he never expressed any negative stance on memecoins or AI-agent token froth in the window. The largest unanticipated theme is privacy/ZK blockchains, which recurs repeatedly but wasn't in the prediction.
